questions.question: List the four necessary conditions for a deadlock.
Compare and contrast centralized and distributed deadlock detection algorithms. Discuss one major advantage and one major disadvantage of each approach.
expectedPoints: Centralized: Simple, single point of analysis. Disadvantage: Single point of failure, potential bottleneck.
Distributed: No single point of failure, more robust. Disadvantage: Higher message complexity, risk of phantom deadlocks.
Explanation of how each algorithm operates at a high level.
Mention of the Deadlock Detection Coordinator (DDC) for centralized and probe messages for distributed (e.g., Chandy-Misra-Haas).
